name: Differential Testing

x.defectClass: Discrepancies between implementations that should agree: spec violations, regressions, and undefined-behaviour divergence.
x.oracle: Another implementation (or a previous version) as the reference — the test fails when outputs diverge on the same input.
x.automationLevel: High — generate or replay inputs and compare outputs automatically.
blindSpots: Bugs shared by all implementations (common-mode) go undetected
Legitimate differences (precision, ordering) cause false alarms
x.cost: Medium — needs multiple implementations and a comparison harness.
x.lifecycleStage: CI / compiler & protocol conformance / security research.

def: Feeding the same inputs to two or more implementations that should agree and flagging any divergence as a likely bug.
problem: Writing an explicit oracle is hard, but multiple implementations of the same spec should produce the same outputs.
mechanism: Generate (often randomly / via fuzzing) a large input space, run it through each implementation, and compare outputs. Divergence localises a bug in one of them or an ambiguity in the spec.
limits: Misses bugs common to all implementations
failures: Treating acceptable precision/ordering diffs as failures
whenNot: Outputs are legitimately non-deterministic without a normal form
myths: "Agreement proves correctness" — both can be wrong in the same way
